$comment: Owner sign-offs for the AGENT-SAFETY META-GAUNTLET (the raccoon rule). A weakening of the gauntlet's own rigor standards is permitted ONLY via an explicit, owner-signed entry here. Each sign-off names the EXACT element being weakened (elementKey), the weakening CLASS it authorizes, the owner, a justification, and an expiry (ISO yyyy-mm-dd; past the run date re-reds). An always-blocking weakening (the placeholder/skip floor) can NEVER be signed (void), and a skip-allowlist-add whose SITE carries a placeholder marker (TODO/FIXME/not implemented/stub/...) is non-signable (it is unfinished work, not a capability gate). The entries below include the branch-local weakenings vs the snapshot's birth commit (d0c214b7) plus Tier 0.2 skipIf conversions (offscreen-canvas, webcodecs, gpu-absent, eacces-untestable-as-root, symlink-unprivileged, fixture-absent, capsule-manifest-absent) plus the clock.ts no-nondeterminism 60/77 line-relocations. The former no-silent-catch benign-catch sign-offs (version.ts/ship.ts/doctor-probes-workspace.ts) were retired when those catches were CURED (given explicit, non-swallowing fallback bodies) and their waivers removed from LITESHIP_WAIVERS — a cure, not a renewed suppression. Derived from the live-vs-birth diff, not hand-guessed.

signoffs.elementKey: skip-allowlist::tests/browser/spsc-ring-browser.test.ts::describe.skipIf(!canUseSAB)('browser SPSCRing with real SharedArrayBuffer and Atomics', () => {
signoffs.justification: Sanctioned capability-gated skip (SharedArrayBuffer / cross-origin isolation (COOP/COEP)). describe.skipIf(!canUseSAB) — the real-SharedArrayBuffer SPSCRing test BODY runs when cross-origin isolation is present and skips only when absent (the in-process ring is covered elsewhere); a runtime capability gate, not a placeholder. Enumerated in SANCTIONED_SKIPS at site granularity; signed vs the snapshot's birth baseline (the bootstrap window).

signoffs.elementKey: skip-allowlist::tests/e2e/astro-directives.e2e.ts::test.skip(!built, 'astro example not built — run: pnpm exec tsx tests/integration/astro/test.ts');
signoffs.justification: Sanctioned capability-gated skip (the built Astro example dist). test.skip(!built, …) — the browser e2e BODY runs when the built Astro example dist is present; the gauntlet runs the integration build before the e2e lane, so CI always exercises it. A runtime capability gate, not a placeholder. Enumerated in SANCTIONED_SKIPS at site granularity; signed vs the snapshot's birth baseline (the bootstrap window).

Sanctioned capability-gated skip (ffmpeg + libx264 on PATH). FFMPEG_RENDER_CAPABLE ? it : it.skip — the render-idempotency BODY runs when ffmpeg+libx264 is on PATH and skips only when absent (the host context unit covers the encode path). A runtime capability gate, not a placeholder. Enumerated in SANCTIONED_SKIPS at site granularity; signed vs the snapshot's birth baseline (the bootstrap window).

Sanctioned capability-gated skip (absence of v8 coverage instrumentation). underCoverage ? it.skip : it — the spawned tsx→vite-server BODY runs except under v8 coverage (a coverage-vs-vite STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION on Windows); the in-process server.test.ts covers the same startDevServer() path under coverage. A runtime instrumentation gate, not a placeholder. Enumerated in SANCTIONED_SKIPS at site granularity; signed vs the snapshot's birth baseline (the bootstrap window).

Sanctioned capability-gated skip (ffmpeg + libx264 on PATH). FFMPEG_RENDER_CAPABLE ? it : it.skip — the scene-render CLI BODY renders an mp4 through ffmpeg+libx264 when the codec is present and skips only when absent. A runtime capability gate, not a placeholder. Enumerated in SANCTIONED_SKIPS at site granularity; signed vs the snapshot's birth baseline (the bootstrap window).

signoffs.justification: Sanctioned capability-gated skip (the loaded Rust WASM kernel). describe.skipIf(!wasmPresent) — the Boundary.evaluateBatch parity property BODY runs when the Rust kernel is loaded and skips only when absent (the scalar evaluate path is covered without it). A runtime capability gate, not a placeholder. Enumerated in SANCTIONED_SKIPS at site granularity; signed vs the snapshot's birth baseline (the bootstrap window).

signoffs.elementKey: skip-allowlist::tests/smoke/intro-render.test.ts::it.skip('skipped — ffmpeg libx264 render probe failed (see liteship doctor)', () => {});
signoffs.justification: Sanctioned capability-gated skip (ffmpeg + libx264 on PATH). the end-to-end intro-scene smoke render BODY runs through ffmpeg+libx264 when present; absent the codec the doctor-probe skips (see `liteship doctor`). The title names the capability (render probe failed), never a TODO — a runtime capability gate, not a placeholder. Enumerated in SANCTIONED_SKIPS at site granularity; signed vs the snapshot's birth baseline (the bootstrap window).

signoffs.elementKey: skip-allowlist::tests/unit/core/wasm-parity.test.ts::describe.skipIf(wasmPresent)('WASM/TS kernel parity (artifact absent — fallbackKernels is what ships here)', () => {
signoffs.justification: Sanctioned capability-gated skip (the wasm artifact being ABSENT). describe.skipIf(wasmPresent) — the INVERSE parity arm BODY runs when the wasm artifact is ABSENT (the fallback is what ships there). A runtime capability gate, not a placeholder. Enumerated in SANCTIONED_SKIPS at site granularity; signed vs the snapshot's birth baseline (the bootstrap window).

Sanctioned capability-gated skip (the staged @liteship/core dist wasm artifact). it.skipIf(!staged) — the module-graph resolution of @liteship/core dist/liteship-compute.wasm BODY runs only when the built artifact is staged (a publish-shape probe). A runtime capability gate, not a placeholder. Enumerated in SANCTIONED_SKIPS at site granularity; signed vs the snapshot's birth baseline (the bootstrap window).

Tier 0.2 — converted silent early-return to describe.skipIf. OffscreenCanvas-dependent browser worker tests BODY runs when OffscreenCanvas is defined; skips only when absent (a runtime capability gate, not a placeholder). Replaces a lying early-return pass.

Tier 0.2 — capability-gated skip for WebCodecs browser lane. describe.skipIf(webcodecsAbsent) — the VideoEncoder/VideoFrame BODY runs when WebCodecs is available; skips only when absent (a runtime capability gate linked to the canonical webcodecsAbsent probe, not a placeholder).

Tier 0.2 — converted silent early-return to it.skipIf. WebGPU runtime tests BODY runs when navigator.gpu is present; skips only when absent (a runtime capability gate, not a placeholder). Replaces a lying early-return pass.

signoffs.elementKey: skip-allowlist::tests/unit/cli/lib/gauntlet-verdict-cache.test.ts::it.skipIf(eaccesUntestableAsRoot)('a cache file with the read bit cleared (EACCES) reads as a MISS, not a throw', () => {
signoffs.justification: Tier 0.2 — converted silent early-return to it.skipIf. EACCES permission test BODY runs only when chmod 000 is testable (non-root); skips on root where EACCES cannot be provoked (a runtime capability gate, not a placeholder).

signoffs.elementKey: skip-allowlist::tests/unit/cli/lib/gauntlet-verdict-cache.test.ts::it.skipIf(eaccesUntestableAsRoot)('a mutation cache file with the read bit cleared (EACCES) reads as a MISS, not a throw', () => {
signoffs.justification: Tier 0.2 — converted silent early-return to it.skipIf. Mutation-cache EACCES test BODY runs only when chmod 000 is testable (non-root); skips on root where EACCES cannot be provoked (a runtime capability gate, not a placeholder).

signoffs.elementKey: skip-allowlist::tests/unit/devops/audit-consumer-mode.test.ts::it.skipIf(symlinkUnprivileged)('resolves the pnpm virtual-store layout via realpath re-seeding', () => {
signoffs.justification: Tier 0.2 — converted silent symlink-privilege return to it.skipIf. The pnpm virtual-store realpath BODY runs when directory symlinks are available and skips only on unprivileged symlink hosts; npm-shaped consumer discovery still covers the non-symlink path.

Tier 0.2 — honest smoke-lane skip for the generated-bench corpus proof. The BODY runs when capsule:compile has produced the manifest; lanes without that manifest skip the catalog-derived proof instead of throwing or silently fabricating sources.
Tier 0.2 — converted fixture-absent early return to it.skipIf. The real RIFF fixture BODY runs when intro-bed.wav is present; synthetic RIFF coverage still runs when the optional asset is absent.

signoffs.elementKey: skip-allowlist::tests/unit/vite/boundary-manifest.test.ts::test.skipIf(symlinkUnprivileged)('scan terminates on circular directory symlinks and still derives the right entries', async () => {
signoffs.justification: Tier 0.2 — converted circular-directory symlink early return to test.skipIf. The traversal BODY runs when directory symlinks are available and skips only on unprivileged symlink hosts; ordinary boundary scanning remains covered.

signoffs.elementKey: skip-allowlist::tests/unit/vite/boundary-manifest.test.ts::test.skipIf(symlinkUnprivileged)('follows symlinked directories to boundary definitions outside the project tree', async () => {
signoffs.justification: Tier 0.2 — converted external-definition symlink early return to test.skipIf. The symlinked boundary definition BODY runs when directory symlinks are available and skips only on unprivileged symlink hosts; boundaryDir override coverage remains the non-symlink proof.

signoffs.elementKey: skip-allowlist::tests/unit/vite/token-manifest.test.ts::test.skipIf(symlinkUnprivileged)('scan terminates on circular directory symlinks and still derives entries', async () => {
signoffs.justification: Tier 0.2 — converted token-manifest circular-directory symlink early return to test.skipIf. The traversal BODY runs when directory symlinks are available and skips only on unprivileged symlink hosts; ordinary token manifest coverage remains active.

Same gauntlet/no-nondeterminism boundary waiver (a sanctioned Date.now() entropy boundary at line 60), RELOCATED by the P4 core-domain migration that moved packages/core/src/clock.ts into packages/core/src/clock/clock.ts — NOT a new deferral. The waiver key embeds the file path, so the pure rename surfaces as remove(old path)+add(new path); the corresponding removal of the packages/core/src/clock.ts|60 waiver is a paired strengthen. No additional code is waived; the same line-60 entropy boundary is waived, only its home moved. Signed vs the base-ref baseline (the P4 restructure window).

Same gauntlet/no-nondeterminism boundary waiver (a sanctioned Date.now() entropy boundary at line 77), RELOCATED by the P4 core-domain migration that moved packages/core/src/clock.ts into packages/core/src/clock/clock.ts — NOT a new deferral. The waiver key embeds the file path, so the pure rename surfaces as remove(old path)+add(new path); the corresponding removal of the packages/core/src/clock.ts|77 waiver is a paired strengthen. No additional code is waived; the same line-77 entropy boundary is waived, only its home moved. Signed vs the base-ref baseline (the P4 restructure window).

Same gauntlet/no-nondeterminism entropy-boundary waiver (the sanctioned seeded-RNG entropy source at line 39), RELOCATED by the P4 core-domain migration from packages/core/src/rng.ts through packages/core/src/internal/rng.ts to its final owner packages/core/src/clock/rng.ts — NOT a new deferral. The waiver key embeds the file path, so the pure rename surfaces as remove(packages/core/src/internal/rng.ts|39)+add(packages/core/src/clock/rng.ts|39); the removal is a paired strengthen. No additional code is waived and the file remains L3; the same line-39 entropy boundary is waived, only its home moved. Signed vs the base-ref baseline (the P4 restructure window).

Glob-membership RESTRUCTURE, not a rigor loss: the P4 core-domain migration split the flat L4 group {receipt,hlc,plan,dag,validated-output,assembly,brands}.ts into the domain-homed L4 globs it now lives under — evidence/{receipt,validated-output}.ts, clock/hlc.ts, authoring/{plan,assembly}.ts, graph/dag.ts, schema/brands.ts (each surfaced as a paired ADD strengthen in the same run). Every path KEPT its L4 assurance band; no file dropped a rigor level. The waiver key embeds the exact glob string, so relocating the files surfaces as remove(old flat glob)+add(new subdir globs). Net rigor unchanged. Signed vs the base-ref baseline (the P4 restructure window).

Glob-membership RESTRUCTURE, not a rigor loss: the former L3 group {boundary,signal,zap,evaluate,gen-frame,speculative,token-buffer,blend,animation,ai-cast,clock,rng}.ts was SPLIT — signal.ts moved into the new reactive-core group {cell-kernel,cell,derived,store,signal,timeline,live-cell}.ts at L4 (a PROMOTION L3→L4; see assurance-map.ts: 'signal moves L4 from its former L3 … a CellKernel-backed value channel, not a bespoke source') plus a new mutation-score floor, and the remaining paths stay L3 under the renamed glob {boundary,zap,evaluate,gen-frame,speculative,token-buffer,blend,animation,ai-cast,clock,rng}.ts (surfaced as the paired ADD strengthen). The waiver key embeds the exact glob string, so editing group membership surfaces as remove(old glob)+add(new glob) — no path lost its rigor band; net rigor INCREASED. Surfaces as a weakening vs origin/main now that the base ref carries the snapshot (the gate moved from birth-baseline to origin/main baseline).

Pure P4 path relocation, not a rigor reduction: every member of this removed flat L3 glob remains L3 under the live domain-owned globs authoring/{boundary,ai-cast}.ts, reactive/{zap,speculative}.ts, media/{gen-frame,token-buffer}.ts, motion/{blend,animation}.ts, clock/clock.ts, and clock/rng.ts. The element key embeds the old flat path, so the relocation appears mechanically as one removal plus paired live strengthen additions. No source file lost assurance coverage.

Pure P4 path relocation, not a rigor reduction: cell-kernel, cell, derived, store, signal, and live-cell remain L4 under packages/core/src/reactive/{cell-kernel,cell,derived,store,signal,live-cell}.ts, while timeline remains L4 under packages/core/src/motion/timeline.ts. The element key embeds the old flat paths, so the domain split appears as a removal plus paired live strengthen additions. No reactive kernel lost assurance coverage.
